prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a emp has a property: it can be activated by pressing the button at a specific position on it. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the emp-analog, and therefore which button on it activates it.

Memory scenario: There are 5 objects on a table: a snig, a yib, a rast, a emp, and a jorp. The snig is underneath the yib. The yib is to-the-left-of the rast. The rast is above the emp. The emp is to-the-right-of the jorp. The snig has a purple button on top, a orange button on side, and a green button on bottom. The yib has a red button on top, a black button on side, and a pink button on bottom. The rast has a purple button on top, a green button on side, and a orange button on bottom. The emp has a cyan button on top, a blue button on side, and a brown button on bottom. The jorp has a black button on top, a red button on side, and a pink button on bottom. Pressing the button on the bottom of the emp activates it.

Perception scenario: There are 5 objects on a shelf: a zop, a dask, a jax, a flen, and a florp. The florp is underneath the zop. The zop is to-the-left-of the jax. The jax is above the flen. The flen is to-the-right-of the dask. The zop has a orange button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. The dask has a orange button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. The jax has a orange button on top, a green button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The flen has a black button on top, a pink button on side, and a red button on bottom. The florp has a cyan button on top, a blue button on side, and a brown button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the emp-analog, and which button activates it? Answer as a fenced JSON block with keys `analog` and `button_color`.
